# MDE v1.4.13

## Features

- Recalibrated all 24 built-in editor themes across the 8 colorway rows and their dark, light-panel, and dark-panel modes for long-form Markdown reading and writing.
- Added stronger technical content layering with a dedicated editor strong-surface token used by code blocks and Mermaid cards, so frontmatter, code, blockquotes, and flowcharts remain visually distinct from the document plane.
- Completed design direction notes for future palettes including Olive Ledger, Fog Ledger, Petrol Archive, and Ash Parchment, keeping them as concepts rather than expanding the current 8 x 3 theme matrix.

## Bug Fixes

- Fixed an editor search and Mermaid preview feedback loop that could trigger React maximum-update-depth errors after switching themes with a Mermaid-heavy document open.
- Excluded derived Mermaid preview SVG text from editor search matching and search mutation tracking, so search remains tied to editable document content.

## Breaking Changes

- None.

## Maintenance

- Added integration coverage that verifies every selectable theme has a complete token block, token/swatches alignment, readable text and muted text contrast, accent/focus visibility, panel/editor separation, and distinct warning/accent colors.
- Added E2E coverage that switches every theme mode against a Markdown fixture with frontmatter, code, Mermaid, and active editor search.
- Kept the theme picker behavior, follow-system preference, persisted dark/light memory, and 8 x 3 theme matrix unchanged.

## Documentation

- User manual screenshots and settings instructions were not changed because this release preserves the existing theme picker flow and only recalibrates existing theme visuals.

## Verification

- `npm run lint`
- `npm run typecheck`
- `npm run test:unit`
- `npm run test:integration`
- `npm run test:e2e`

## Artifacts

- GitHub Actions publishes macOS DMG/ZIP and Windows NSIS artifacts for this version.
- Optional macOS signing and notarization secrets were not listed in repository secrets during preflight, so release automation may use the unsigned macOS artifact fallback.
